frameworks.name: disentangled representation learning
frameworks.one_line: neural network separates shared vs private latent variables across paired views

frameworks.name: multi-view latent variable models
frameworks.one_line: extends shared-variance methods to nonlinear, geometry-preserving private/shared decomposition

frameworks.name: manifold geometry preservation
frameworks.one_line: latent representations retain geometric structure of underlying view manifolds

core_question: How can we infer disentangled, interpretable shared and private latent variables from paired high-dimensional multi-view data while preserving geometry?
key_assumptions: paired samples across views share some latent sources and have private ones
geometric structure of latents carries interpretive value
nonlinear mappings are needed to capture cross-view relationships
manipulations: compared SPLICE against existing multi-view methods
varied a priori estimates of latent dimensionality
tested on data with mixed shared and private structure
key_findings: SPLICE disentangles shared and private representations more effectively than baselines
yields more interpretable representations by preserving geometry
more robust to incorrect a priori latent dimensionality estimates
interpretations: SPLICE is a general-purpose tool for succinct, interpretable descriptions of paired datasets
characterizing shared vs private structure in multi-view recordings reveals system-level insights
